title: Provide a way to use use cifs (and nfs) mounted through autofs from rootless podman
body:
### Feature request description

Provide a way to use use cifs (and nfs) mounted through autofs from rootless podman.

I posted a question about using cifs/nfs from rootless podman at:

* https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/25814
* [fedora discussions: Is there any sane way to use use cifs (or nfs) from rootless podman?](https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/t/is-there-any-sane-way-to-use-use-cifs-or-nfs-from-rootless-podman/148202)

There has been _no answers_. Hence I conclude the problem is _unsolved_.

### Suggest potential solution

* cifs/nfs volumes mounted on the host system (and are accessible to the host user) should be accessible to podman containers somehow
* for details why this is currently _not_ possible refer to my posted questions (where you could find full details)
* unsure if this is (only) a SELinux problem

### Have you considered any alternatives?

At first glance it seems that using the local volume driver like this could provide a solution:

```bash
podman volume create --driver local --opt type=nfs --opt "o=nfsvers=4,minorversion=1,ro" --opt "device=192.168.10.104:/" mnt-ro
```

However, this is not possible with rootless podman (as mounts are only allowed for root).

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
This isn't anything podman can or cannot do specifically.
First as you mention as rootless you cannot mount smb/nfs and most other file systems. So all you can do is mount on the host and then bind mount it. In that case you need to ensure the proper permissions, e.g. right uid/gids, selinux, etc...

So overall this isn't a feature or a bug, it is fair to ask for help in https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/25814 but keep in mind our time is limited so there is no guarantee that anybody will look at it and can help you.

A viable solution for this is at https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/25814

Thank you for support!
